rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in valid component type scopes, using only visible feature names and constants.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers or property references not present in the AADL model.
generic_rules: Use only visible AADL features or helper declarations within the same annex for AGREE expressions.
Ensure type consistency between assumed types and actual expression types in AGREE contracts.
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as typed ports or constants.
Write output dependency guarantees that relate visible inputs to visible outputs, avoiding implementation-only subcomponent paths.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks within component type or implementation scopes.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference named constants or visible feature names, not implementation-specific identifiers.
anti_patterns: Do not reference identifiers or properties that are not visible or declared in the same AADL component scope.
Do not attempt to use AADL property values directly as AGREE variables without explicit model exposure.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
